name: Antiques Ontology (Antiques and Insurance Valuations)
description: Ontology modelling antique items, professional valuations, provenance tracking, insurance policies, claims, and appraiser qualifications for comprehensive antiques insurance management.

components.AntiqueItem: A collectible object of historical significance with category, period, condition, and materials
components.Valuation: Professional assessment of monetary worth for insurance or sale purposes
components.Appraiser: Qualified professional who assesses and certifies value and authenticity
components.InsurancePolicy: Contract providing financial protection against loss, damage, or theft
components.Owner: Individual or organization legally possessing antique items
components.Provenance: Documented history of ownership and custody from origin to present
components.Claim: Formal request for compensation under an insurance policy

versionHistory.changes: Initial antiques and insurance valuations ontology. 7 entities (AntiqueItem, Valuation, Appraiser, InsurancePolicy, Owner, Provenance, Claim), 9 relationships, 6 business rules. Covers item cataloguing, professional appraisal, provenance tracking, insurance coverage, and claims management.

notes: ANTIQUES-ONT models the antiques insurance valuation domain for BAIV. Covers the full lifecycle from item acquisition and provenance verification through professional appraisal and insurance coverage to claims processing.
